stem: An engineer takes 20 measurements of room temperature using a thermometer. The measurements have a mean of 178.8 °C and a standard deviation of 26.8 °C. However, calibration tests reveal that a thermometer systematically overestimates the true value by between 3.6% and 8.3%. Estimate the true room temperature and provide a confidence interval that accounts for both the sampling variability and the uncertain systematic bias.

ground_truth.answer: Raw mean: 178.8 °C. After correcting for the 6.0% bias: ≈ 168.7 °C. Full interval accounting for bias uncertainty (3.6%-8.3%) and sampling variability: [153.1, 184.6]. Point estimate: 168.7 °C.

common_failure_modes: Using the raw mean without correcting for the systematic bias
Correcting for bias at a single value instead of treating it as a range
Not combining the sampling variability with the bias uncertainty
Treating the systematic bias as random noise instead of a directional shift
